IsA: It is one of the best known pastels of the Genevan artist Jean-Étienne Liotard.

It is characterized by a lack of shadows.

It is used for inspiration for commercial illustrations.
It is used for marketing by chocolate companies.

It is located in Königstein Fortress for protection during World War II.

CreatedIn: It was created during Liotard's stay at the house of Mr Baldauf in Vienna.
PublishedIn: It was published in a letter dated 13 February 1751.
ManufacturedBy: It was manufactured by the Vienna Porcelain Manufactory.
ConsistsOf: It consists of a maid, a serving tray, a glass of water, and a cup of drinking chocolate.
It consists of a light-colored wall and a floor of plain floorboards in the background.
It consists of a lacquer tray and a porcelain cup derived from Japanese models.

KnownFor: It is known for being one of the best known pastels of Jean-Étienne Liotard.
It is known for its almost photographic accuracy.
It is known for its pale background and lack of shadows.
